danger_signals.visual_form: Animated hostile creatures in level spaces
danger_signals.meaning: Areas with enemies signal hazard zones requiring evasion or combat
Lethal stage architecture that damages player on contact
End-of-level threat requiring defeat to progress

safety_signals.meaning: Safe traversal zones with no immediate threat

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Distinct enemy sprite designs per level theme
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Enemy spawn points correlate with level architecture

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Standard platformer level layout with enemies, platforms, and collectibles
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies patrol or attack on player proximity; platforms remain stable

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Large boss sprite dominates screen space; arena constricts or features specialized arena design
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss executes distinctive attack patterns; player must adapt strategy

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Environmental aesthetics shift (beach→underwater→urban, etc.) with corresponding visual palette changes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy types, hazard configurations, and difficulty scaling adjust to theme

environmental_storytelling.example: Beach setting establishes casual surfing context; subsequent levels reveal escalating adventure scope through visual environment transitions
Aquatic enemies in water-themed stages; terrestrial/mechanical enemies in urban zones suggest world progression narrative
Stage-to-stage background and palette shifts communicate narrative geography without dialogue

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Platform traversal, jumping, running; sprite animation reflects direction and speed
Knockback animation; temporary invulnerability flash; reduced mobility
Animated loop movement within designated patrol zone
Accelerated movement toward player; attack animation triggered on proximity
Cyclical attack patterns; phase transitions on damage thresholds; screen-dominant positioning

notes: Greendog operates within classic 16-bit platformer visual language. World-state communication relies on environmental theming and enemy type variation rather than explicit UI indicators. Danger/safety signals are implicit in platformer conventions (hazard placement, enemy positioning). Limited faction complexity—primarily player vs. hostile world entities. Manifestation layer emphasizes visual theme consistency and behavioral enemy AI patterns.
